_about: BT boost-regulator OUTPUT loop: from boost VOUT (REG-BT pin 13) to its nearest output cap (C4A-BT pad 1, VOUT-BT), return through the continuous bottom GND plane. This is the output-cap connection inductance that sets the VOUT-BT overshoot/ring. Coordinates in board mm, from the PnP file + the VOUT-BT net flood-fill.

forward._note: A point on the VOUT-BT pour (orange in out/vout_bt_region.png).

return._note: A point on the continuous bottom GND plane.

_terminal_note: Port at the boost OUTPUT pin EDGE (REG-BT VOUT, where copper exits the IC = closest external point): A on VOUT-BT, B on the GND plane directly beneath. This excludes the IC's internal pin and measures the external PCB loop only.

closure._note: ALT CLOSURE (sensitivity study): cap C4A-BT VOUT PAD at (44.704,29.005) instead of the GND pad (44.704,31.955). Ties VOUT to the GND plane directly beneath the VOUT pad -> idealized vertical drop, ignoring the ~2.95mm lateral run to the cap GND pad. Baseline config is config_vout_bt.json.

freq._note: extra includes 500kHz = the regulator switching frequency. Used by the legacy single-run gerber_inductance.py only; sweep_inductance.py uses `sweep`.

sweep._note: Closure-sensitivity study: single point (designated mesh @ 500kHz) with the ALT (VOUT-pad) closure. Compare L against the baseline config.
